frictions.description: Agent-browser CDP file upload does not provide File.path property which is required by Electron's file handling
frictions.resolution: None available through browser automation - requires native Electron file dialog or direct file system access

blockers.description: App crashes with 'Cannot read properties of undefined (reading split)' when a file is added. The crash occurs in the ffprobe processing because the file path is undefined when uploaded via CDP. This is a fundamental incompatibility between browser-based file upload (used by agent-browser) and Electron's native file path handling.
blockers.quickFixAttempted: Attempted multiple file upload methods: (1) Direct upload to input[type=file], (2) Click on drop zone to trigger native file dialog, (3) File drag-drop simulation. All methods result in undefined file path.

summary: Tested 0 assertions: 0 passed, 0 failed, 6 blocked. All quality preset assertions (VAL-QUAL-001 through VAL-QUAL-006) are blocked due to a bug in the app where it crashes when files are added via browser-based file upload. The File.path property is not available when using CDP-based file upload, causing ffprobe to receive an undefined file path. This is an Electron-specific issue where the native file path extension on the File object is not populated through browser-based file handling.
